@licensing.about.srTitle.description: Visually-hidden accessible title of the About dialog, read by screen readers. The dialog has no visible title; the app name below serves as the visual heading. 'Cmdr' is the product name and must NOT be translated.

@licensing.about.appName.description: The product name, shown large as the visual heading of the About dialog. A brand name; do NOT translate.

@licensing.about.tagline.description: One-line product tagline under the app name in the About dialog.

@licensing.about.version.description: Version line in the About dialog. '(open beta)' flags the current release stage.

licensing.about.betaNote: Found something bad? Tell me on <github>GitHub</github>. I read every report!

@licensing.about.betaNote.description: Friendly first-person beta note in the About dialog inviting bug reports. Spoken as David (the author), so keep it warm and personal. <github> wraps the inline link to the GitHub issues page; 'GitHub' is a brand name and must NOT be translated.

licensing.about.noLicense: No license – only personal use allowed

@licensing.about.noLicense.description: License-status line in the About dialog for a personal/free or expired install. Neutral, non-judgmental tone. The dash is an en dash (–).

@licensing.about.perpetual.description: License-status line in the About dialog for a perpetual (one-time) commercial license.

placeholders.org: the licensed organization name, or a fallback phrase like 'your organization'

@licensing.about.commercial.description: License-status line in the About dialog for a commercial subscription with no known expiry date.

placeholders.org: the licensed organization name, or a fallback phrase like 'your organization'

@licensing.about.commercialUntil.description: License-status line in the About dialog for a commercial subscription with a known expiry date.

placeholders.org: the licensed organization name, or a fallback phrase like 'your organization'
placeholders.date: the expiry date, already formatted for the user's locale (e.g. June 15, 2026)

@licensing.about.fallbackOrg.description: Fallback used in place of the organization name when none is recorded, e.g. 'Commercial license for your organization'. Must read naturally where '{org}' is substituted in the other About license lines.

licensing.about.aiAttribution: AI powered by Falcon-H1R-7B by Technology Innovation Institute (TII)

@licensing.about.aiAttribution.description: Attribution line crediting the bundled AI model. 'Falcon-H1R-7B', 'Technology Innovation Institute', and 'TII' are proper names and must NOT be translated; only the 'AI powered by' framing is translatable.

@licensing.about.linkWebsite.description: Footer link label in the About dialog pointing to the product website.

@licensing.about.linkGetLicense.description: Footer link label in the About dialog pointing to the pricing page. Shown only for personal/expired installs.

@licensing.about.linkGithub.description: Footer link label in the About dialog pointing to the source repository. 'GitHub' is a brand name and must NOT be translated.

@licensing.about.linkDiscord.description: Footer link label in the About dialog pointing to the community chat. 'Discord' is a brand name and must NOT be translated.

@licensing.about.copyright.description: Copyright line at the foot of the About dialog. 'David Veszelovszki' is a personal name; the year range stays literal.

licensing.commercialReminder.title: Thanks for using Cmdr!

@licensing.commercialReminder.title.description: Title of the periodic reminder modal shown to personal-license users. Warm and grateful, not nagging. 'Cmdr' is the product name; do NOT translate.

@licensing.commercialReminder.usingPerso.description: First line of the commercial-reminder modal stating the current license tier. 'Personal' is a license tier name; keep it capitalized and consistent with the other license labels.

licensing.commercialReminder.askCommerci: If you''re using Cmdr at work, please get a Commercial license to stay compliant.

@licensing.commercialReminder.askCommerc.description: Body of the commercial-reminder modal politely asking work users to buy a commercial license. 'Cmdr' is the product name; 'Commercial' is a license tier name.

licensing.commercialReminder.priceInfo: Commercial licenses are $59/year/user and support continued development.

@licensing.commercialReminder.priceInfo.description: Supporting line in the commercial-reminder modal giving the price and framing it as support for development. Keep the price '$59/year/user' literal (currency and amount must not change).

licensing.commercialReminder.declinePers: I only use Cmdr<break></break>for personal purposes

@licensing.commercialReminder.declinePer.description: Secondary (dismiss) button in the commercial-reminder modal, for users who only use the app personally. <break> is an inline line break for two-line layout in a narrow button; place it at a sensible wrap point for the target language. 'Cmdr' is the product name.

@licensing.commercialReminder.getCommerc.description: Primary button in the commercial-reminder modal that opens the pricing page. Keep it short (a button).

licensing.expiration.title: Your commercial license has expired

@licensing.expiration.title.description: Title of the modal shown once when a commercial subscription has expired.

@licensing.expiration.orgName.description: Line in the expiration modal naming the organization the expired license belonged to. <strong> emphasizes the organization name.

@licensing.expiration.message.description: Main line of the expiration modal stating when the subscription lapsed. <strong> emphasizes the date.

placeholders.date: the expiry date, already formatted for the user's locale (e.g. March 1, 2026)

licensing.expiration.info: Cmdr is now running in personal use mode. If you''re still using it for work, please renew your license.

@licensing.expiration.info.description: Reassuring follow-up in the expiration modal explaining the app keeps working (now in personal mode) and inviting renewal. 'Cmdr' is the product name; do NOT translate.

@licensing.expiration.renew.description: Primary button in the expiration modal that opens the renewal page. Keep it short (a button).

@licensing.expiration.continue.description: Secondary button in the expiration modal that dismisses it and keeps the app in personal mode. Keep it short (a button).

@licensing.dialog.loading.description: Title of the license dialog while it loads the current license info. A trailing-ellipsis loading state.

@licensing.dialog.detailsTitle.description: Title of the license dialog when an existing license is present (shows its details).

@licensing.dialog.enterTitle.description: Title of the license dialog when no license is present (the key-entry form).

licensing.dialog.serverInvalidBanner: This key couldn''t be verified with the server. Please try a different key or email us at <supportEmail>{email}</supportEmail>.

@licensing.dialog.serverInvalidBanner.description: Warning banner in the license-details view shown when a stored key was later rejected by the server during re-validation. <supportEmail> wraps the inline support-email link.

placeholders.email: the support email address (shown as the link text), e.g. hello@getcmdr.com

@licensing.dialog.labelType.description: Row label in the license-details view for the license type. Pairs with the type value (e.g. 'Commercial subscription').

@licensing.dialog.labelOrganization.description: Row label in the license-details view for the licensed organization name.

@licensing.dialog.labelValidity.description: Row label in the license-details view for the license validity/expiry status.

@licensing.dialog.labelKey.description: Row label in the license-details view for the short license-key code.

@licensing.dialog.typeCommercialPerpetua.description: License-type value shown in the details view for a one-time perpetual commercial license. 'Commercial' is a license tier name.

@licensing.dialog.typeCommercialSubscrip.description: License-type value shown in the details view for a recurring commercial subscription. 'Commercial' is a license tier name.

@licensing.dialog.validityNotYetVerified.description: Validity value shown (in yellow) for a commercial license whose server check hasn't completed yet. Paired with the hint about verifying within a few days.

@licensing.dialog.validityPerpetualUntil.description: Validity value for a perpetual license that includes updates until a given date. The em-style dash separates 'Perpetual' from the updates clause; keep a similar separator.

placeholders.date: the updates-cutoff date, already formatted for the user's locale (e.g. June 15, 2026)

@licensing.dialog.validityPerpetual.description: Validity value for a perpetual license with no known updates-cutoff date.

@licensing.dialog.validityValidUntil.description: Validity value for a commercial subscription valid until a given date.

placeholders.date: the expiry date, already formatted for the user's locale (e.g. June 15, 2026)

@licensing.dialog.validityActive.description: Validity value for a commercial license that is active with no specific expiry date shown.

@licensing.dialog.validityExpiredOn.description: Validity value (in red) for a license that has expired.

placeholders.date: the expiry date, already formatted for the user's locale (e.g. June 15, 2026)

licensing.dialog.pendingHint: We''ll verify with the server automatically within {days} days.

@licensing.dialog.pendingHint.description: Hint under the 'Not yet verified' validity row reassuring the user the app will re-check automatically soon. Always 2 or more days in practice, so plain plural is fine.

placeholders.days: the number of days until automatic re-verification (currently 7)

@licensing.dialog.useDifferentKey.description: Secondary button in the license-details view that starts the deactivate/re-enter flow. Keep it short (a button).

@licensing.dialog.close.description: Primary button that closes the license dialog. Keep it short (a button).

licensing.dialog.resetConfirm: This will deactivate your current license on this device. You can reactivate anytime with a valid key.

@licensing.dialog.resetConfirm.description: Confirmation copy shown before deactivating the current license. Reassures the user it's reversible.

@licensing.dialog.cancel.description: Secondary button that backs out of an action in the license dialog (reset confirmation, key entry). Keep it short (a button).

@licensing.dialog.continue.description: Primary button confirming the license-deactivation step. Keep it short (a button).

licensing.dialog.enterPrompt: Paste your license key from the email you received after purchase. Don''t have one yet? <getLicense>Get a license</getLicense>.

@licensing.dialog.enterPrompt.description: Instruction above the key-entry field telling the user where to find their key. <getLicense> wraps the inline link to the pricing page.

@licensing.dialog.inputPlaceholder.description: Placeholder text inside the license-key input showing the short-code format. 'CMDR-ABCD-EFGH-1234' is a format example and must NOT be translated; only the 'Example:' label is translatable.

licensing.dialog.retryExhausted: We''ve tried {count} times and it didn''t work. We''re sorry for the trouble. Please drop us a message at <supportEmail>{email}</supportEmail> and we''ll sort it out.

@licensing.dialog.retryExhausted.description: Help text shown after several failed activation attempts (3 or more) against a server-rejected key, apologizing and pointing to support. Warm and apologetic. <supportEmail> wraps the inline support-email link.

placeholders.count: how many times activation was attempted (always 3 or more here)
placeholders.email: the support email address (shown as the link text), e.g. hello@getcmdr.com

licensing.dialog.serverInvalidHelp: If you believe this is a mistake, email us at <supportEmail>{email}</supportEmail> and we''ll sort it out.

@licensing.dialog.serverInvalidHelp.description: Help text shown after a server-rejected key (fewer than 3 attempts) inviting the user to contact support. <supportEmail> wraps the inline support-email link.

placeholders.email: the support email address (shown as the link text), e.g. hello@getcmdr.com

licensing.dialog.genericHelp: If you need help, contact us at <supportEmail>{email}</supportEmail>.

@licensing.dialog.genericHelp.description: Generic help text shown under an activation error inviting the user to contact support. <supportEmail> wraps the inline support-email link.

placeholders.email: the support email address (shown as the link text), e.g. hello@getcmdr.com

@licensing.dialog.cancelButton.description: Secondary button in the key-entry form that cancels and closes the dialog. Keep it short (a button).

@licensing.dialog.checking.description: Primary-button label while re-checking a previously server-rejected key. A transient progress state with a trailing ellipsis.

@licensing.dialog.tryAgain.description: Primary-button label to retry activation after a failure. Keep it short (a button).

@licensing.dialog.activating.description: Primary-button label while a license key is being activated. A transient progress state with a trailing ellipsis.

@licensing.dialog.activate.description: Primary-button label that submits the entered license key for activation. Keep it short (a button).

licensing.dialog.activatedToast: License activated. Thanks for your support! ❤️

@licensing.dialog.activatedToast.description: Success toast shown after activating a license when no organization name is known. Warm and grateful; keep the heart emoji.

licensing.dialog.activatedToastNamed: Welcome aboard, {org}! Thanks for your support. ❤️

@licensing.dialog.activatedToastNamed.description: Success toast shown after activating a license when an organization name is known. Warm and welcoming; keep the heart emoji.

licensing.dialog.networkFallbackError: Couldn''t verify your license with the server. Please try again later.

@licensing.dialog.networkFallbackError.description: Error shown when the server can't be reached and the license type doesn't allow an optimistic fallback. Conversational; never use the words 'error' or 'failed'.

@licensing.dialog.expiredOnError.description: Inline error shown when the entered key is valid but the subscription has expired.

placeholders.date: the expiry date, already formatted for the user's locale (e.g. June 15, 2026)

licensing.dialog.expiredOnHelp: You can renew your subscription or purchase a new license at getcmdr.com.

@licensing.dialog.expiredOnHelp.description: Help text under the expired-key error pointing to the website to renew or buy. 'getcmdr.com' is a domain and must NOT be translated.

licensing.dialog.serverInvalidError: We know this key but when we checked it with our payment provider, it didn''t recognize it. This can happen if the purchase was refunded or not cleared.

@licensing.dialog.serverInvalidError.description: Inline error shown when a key is cryptographically valid but the payment provider doesn't recognize the transaction (e.g. refunded). Conversational and non-blaming; never use the words 'error' or 'failed'.

@licensing.dialog.emptyKeyError.description: Inline error shown when the user tries to activate with an empty key field.

@licensing.dialog.mailtoSubject.description: Prefilled subject line of the support email opened from the license dialog's contact links.

@licensing.dialog.mailtoBody.description: Prefilled body of the support email opened from the license dialog's contact links. Keep the literal newline escapes (\n) for line breaks; {key} is filled in with the user's license key.

placeholders.key: the license key the user entered (or their stored short code)

licensing.error.badSignature: This license key failed our signature verification, meaning that it doesn''t look like a valid key.

@licensing.error.badSignature.description: Activation-error message when the key fails Ed25519 signature verification. Paired with its help hint. Conversational; never use the words 'error' or 'failed' ('failed our signature verification' here describes the check result, kept from the original copy).

licensing.error.badSignatureHint: Please double-check for typos and try pasting it again from your purchase email.

@licensing.error.badSignatureHint.description: Help hint paired with the bad-signature activation error, suggesting a re-paste from the purchase email.

licensing.error.badFormat: Our license key format is different from this.

@licensing.error.badFormat.description: Activation-error message when the entered text doesn't match any known key format. Paired with its help hint.

licensing.error.badFormatHint: License keys are either a short code (CMDR-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX) or a longer cryptographic key from your purchase email.

@licensing.error.badFormatHint.description: Help hint paired with the bad-format activation error, describing the two valid key shapes. 'CMDR-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX' is a format example and must NOT be translated.

licensing.error.shortCodeNotFound: This license looks good, but we looked hard and couldn''t find it in our database.

@licensing.error.shortCodeNotFound.description: Activation-error message when a well-formed short code isn't found server-side. Friendly and slightly apologetic. Paired with its help hint.

licensing.error.shortCodeNotFoundHint: Please verify that you pasted the correct key from your purchase email.

@licensing.error.shortCodeNotFoundHint.description: Help hint paired with the short-code-not-found error, asking the user to re-check the pasted key.

licensing.error.network: Ouch, we couldn''t reach the license server this time.

@licensing.error.network.description: Activation-error message when the license server is unreachable. Light, friendly tone ('Ouch'). Paired with its help hint; never use the words 'error' or 'failed'.

licensing.error.networkHint: Please check your internet connection and try again.

@licensing.error.networkHint.description: Help hint paired with the network activation error, suggesting a connection check and retry.

licensing.error.server: Hmm, the license server responded with something weird. We''re sorry about that.

@licensing.error.server.description: Activation-error message when the license server returns an unexpected response. Casual and apologetic ('Hmm'). Paired with its help hint.

@licensing.error.serverHint.description: Help hint paired with the server activation error, suggesting a later retry.

licensing.error.generic: Something went wrong when activating this key.

@licensing.error.generic.description: Fallback activation-error message for an unclassified failure. Conversational; never use the words 'error' or 'failed'.

licensing.error.genericHint: Please try again. If the problem persists, email us and we''ll help.

@licensing.error.genericHint.description: Help hint paired with the generic activation error, suggesting a retry and offering email support.

licensing.section.title: License

@licensing.section.title.description: Heading of the License section in the settings window.

@licensing.section.loading.description: Placeholder shown in the settings License section while the license info loads. A trailing-ellipsis loading state.

@licensing.section.labelType.description: Row label in the settings License section for the license type.

@licensing.section.labelOrganization.description: Row label in the settings License section for the licensed organization name.

@licensing.section.labelStatus.description: Row label in the settings License section for the license status/validity.

@licensing.section.labelKey.description: Row label in the settings License section for the short license-key code.

@licensing.section.manageKey.description: Button in the settings License section (shown when a license exists) that opens the license dialog. Keep it short (a button).

@licensing.section.enterKey.description: Button in the settings License section (shown when no license exists) that opens the key-entry dialog. Keep it short (a button).

@licensing.section.getLicense.description: Button in the settings License section (shown when no license exists) that opens the pricing page. Keep it short (a button).

@licensing.section.typePersonal.description: License-type value in the settings License section for a free personal install. 'Personal' is a license tier name; keep the '(free)' parenthetical.

@licensing.section.typeCommercialPerpetu.description: License-type value in the settings License section for a one-time perpetual commercial license. 'Commercial' is a license tier name.

@licensing.section.typeCommercialSubscri.description: License-type value in the settings License section for a recurring commercial subscription. 'Commercial' is a license tier name.

@licensing.section.statusExpiredOn.description: Status value (in red) in the settings License section for an expired license.

placeholders.date: the expiry date, already formatted for the user's locale (e.g. June 15, 2026)

@licensing.section.statusUpdatesUntil.description: Status value in the settings License section for a perpetual license with an updates cutoff.

placeholders.date: the updates-cutoff date, already formatted for the user's locale (e.g. June 15, 2026)

@licensing.section.statusValidUntil.description: Status value in the settings License section for a subscription valid until a given date.

placeholders.date: the expiry date, already formatted for the user's locale (e.g. June 15, 2026)

@licensing.section.statusActive.description: Status value in the settings License section for an active license with no specific date shown.
